title: Rajya Sabha elections announce four winning candidates in Odisha - News On AIR
full_text: The results of the Rajya Sabha elections from Odisha were declared today, with four candidates emerging victorious in a closely watched contest. Mr Manmohan Samal and Mr Sujeet Kumar have been elected to the Upper House as BJP nominees. Former Union Minister Dilip Ray, who was backed by the BJP, also registered a notable victory as an Independent candidate, defeating Dr Datteswar Hota, who was the common candidate of the BJD, Congress and CPI (M). A few MLAs from the opposition BJD and Congress had cross-voted in favour of Mr Ray, defying the party whip. Mr Santrupt Mishra of the BJD has also been declared elected, ensuring the regional party’s continued representation in the Upper House.

title: Amit Shah appointed as BJP’s observer for West Bengal - News On AIR
full_text:
The BJP has appointed senior party leader and Union Minister Amit Shah as Central Observer for the election of the Leader of the Legislative Party in West Bengal.
Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has been appointed as the Central Co-observer in the state.
While Union Minister J P Nadda has been appointed as Central Observer for the election of the Leader of the Legislative Party in Assam.
Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini has been appointed as the Central Co-Observer in the state.
Union Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya and BJP’s Puducherry in-charge Nirmal Kumar Surana have been appointed as Central Observers for the election of the Leader of the Legislative Party in Puducherry.
These names were approved by the BJP Parliamentary Board for Central Observers.
